name: Chicken Chowmein
description: Street-style chicken chowmein with al dente noodles, seasoned chicken, crunchy vegetables, soy sauce, pepper, garlic, and MSG.
recipeIngredient: 2 to 3 tablespoons sunflower oil, plus more for boiling and coating noodles
70 grams onions, cut into half-moon slices
25 grams spring onions, diagonally cut, plus 10 grams for garnish
2 tablespoons dark soy sauce, plus 2 teaspoons for chicken
1 tablespoon white pepper, plus 1 tablespoon for chicken
1 tablespoon black pepper, plus 1 tablespoon for chicken

itemListElement.text: Bring a large vessel of water to a boil.
Add 2 to 3 tablespoons oil to the boiling water.
Add the noodles and cook until al dente.
Strain the noodles and immediately rinse under cold water.
Add a little oil to the noodles in the strainer and mix properly to prevent sticking. Set aside.
Add 1 tablespoon white pepper, 1 tablespoon black pepper, and 2 teaspoons dark soy sauce.
Cook until the chicken is fully done. Set aside.
Add the garlic and cook until golden brown.
Keep the heat high and add the onions, cabbage, carrot, and spring onions.
Fry the vegetables for 30 to 45 seconds.
Add the cooked noodles and cooked chicken.
Add dark soy sauce, MSG, white pepper, and black pepper.
Toss everything well over medium-high heat until evenly coated and heated through.
Garnish with the remaining 10 grams spring onions and serve hot.
